title: 11 Herbal Infusions to Permanently Cure Itchy Eyes

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy eyes because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and ease irritation, making it a natural relief for itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis decoctions can help with itchy eyes. The plant's active compounds, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This reduces irritation and discomfort. The decoctions also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties, helping to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis has a soothing effect on the eyes, calming the tissues and reducing itching. This makes it a popular remedy for itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or other irritants. When used as a decoction, Euphrasia officinalis can be applied as eye drops or compresses to directly treat itchy eyes. Its natural and gentle properties make it a suitable option for people looking for a non-pharmacological solution to itchy eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction to help with itchy eyes, start by gathering a handful of the plant's fresh leaves and flowers, if available. Rinse the plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, combine the plant material with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the plant's properties to infuse into the water. After the simmering time has passed, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly. Next, strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Finally, let the decoction cool completely before storing it in the refrigerator for later use. To use the decoction, simply soak a clean cloth in it and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes to help relieve itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, is a plant used for centuries to treat various health issues. The decoctions made from its flowers are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. When applied to itchy eyes, these decoctions can help soothe the discomfort and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in calendula can calm the irritated tissues and reduce swelling around the eyes. This can provide relief from the itchy sensation and discomfort associated with irritated eyes. The antioxidants present in calendula can also help protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals. The plant's antimicrobial properties may also help prevent infections that can cause eye irritation. Using calendula decoctions for itchy eyes may also promote healing and reduce the risk of scarring.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering a handful of dried Plantago lanceolata leaves, also known as ribwort or buckthorn. Rinse the leaves with cold water, then chop them into small pieces. In a medium saucepan, combine the chopped leaves with 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. As the decoction cooks, the plant's natural oils and compounds will infuse into the water, creating a soothing and anti-inflammatory liquid. Once the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use it, simply dip a clean cloth into the decoction and apply it to your closed eyes, allowing the liquid to absorb and help calm the itchiness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoction is a natural remedy that may help with itchy eyes. The plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. This can help to calm itchy eyes and make them feel more comfortable. Echinacea purpurea also has antihistamine properties, which can help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that can cause itchy eyes. When histamine levels are lower, it can help to reduce the urge to rub or scratch the eyes, which can make the itching worse. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation, blocking histamine, and protecting the eyes from damage, Echinacea purpurea decoction may provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant. If using fresh leaves, rinse them under cold water to remove any dirt. Chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a saucepan. Add about two cups of water for every one cup of plant material. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Allow the decoction to cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container. Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. To use the decoction, soak a clean cloth in it and apply it to the itchy eye area for relief. You can also add a few drops of the decoction to your eye, but be sure to dilute it with water first and do a patch test to ensure you're not sensitive to it.

When you use Symphytum officinale decoction for itchy eyes, it can give you relief from the discomfort and itching, and help you see more clearly. The decoction can be applied as eye drops or used as a compress on the eyes to get relief from itchy eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction to help with itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as ribwort or lanceleaf plantain. You can find it in fields, meadows, or even in your own backyard. Choose a clean pot and boil about a cup of water, then add a handful of the leaves to the water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the plant's healing properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, remove the leaves and let the decoction cool down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then use the cooled decoction as an eye wash by gently pouring it into your eyes to soothe and calm the itchiness. You can do this 2-3 times a day, or as needed, to get relief from itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis decoctions, also known as aloe vera gel, can help with itchy eyes. The gel contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that calm the eyes and reduce swelling. When applied to itchy eyes, aloe vera gel can bring down the heat and redness, making the eyes feel cooler and more comfortable. The gel can also help soothe the itchy sensation by reducing the inflammation and irritation that causes it. Aloe vera has been used for centuries to treat various eye problems, including dryness and irritation, making it a popular natural remedy for itchy eyes. The gel is also easy to apply and can be used in combination with other eye drops or ointments for better results.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried Plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers. Rinse the plant material under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris, then place it in a saucepan with 2 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the plant material has lost its color. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties of the Plantago lanceolata to infuse into the liquid. Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the plant material. Let the decoction cool to room temperature, then use it as an eye wash to help soothe and calm itchy eyes. You can also refrigerate or freeze the decoction for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita decoctions are good for itchy eyes because they have anti-inflammatory properties. This means they can reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, making them feel cooler and more comfortable. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that can help with itchy eyes, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as ribwort or lungwort. Begin by rinsing the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or pot. Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, allowing the plant's active properties to infuse into the water. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves, and discard the solids. The resulting decoction can be cooled and used as an eye wash to help soothe and calm itchy eyes. It's recommended to refrigerate the decoction for later use, and to use clean cotton balls or pads to apply the cooled liquid directly to the eyes, or to soak a clean cloth in the decoction and place it over the eyes for a few minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, is a plant with many health benefits. Its decoctions, or liquid extracts, can be used to soothe itchy eyes. When made into a decoction, yarrow's active ingredients can be easily absorbed by the body, providing quick relief from itchy eyes. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that helps with itchy eyes, you'll need some fresh or dried Plantago lanceolata leaves. First, rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine one tablespoon of the leaves with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the leaves by straining the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean bowl. If using fresh leaves, rinse them thoroughly with clean water and pat them dry with a paper towel. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan. This means it can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, which are common causes of itchy eyes. The decoction also has antiseptic properties, which help fight off bacteria and other germs that can cause infections and irritation in the eyes. Plantago lanceolata has been traditionally used to treat various eye problems, including conjunctivitis and blepharitis. The decoction is thought to work by soothing and calming the eyes, reducing the urge to rub or scratch them, which can further irritate the eyes and make the problem worse. As a natural remedy, Plantago lanceolata decoctions may be a good option for people who want to avoid using harsh chemicals or prescription medications to treat itchy eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Plantago lanceolata herbal decoction that helps with itchy eyes, start by gathering the fresh or dried leaves of the plantain plant. If using fresh leaves, pick them from a clean and pesticide-free area, and wash them with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan, and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. You can also use dried plantain leaves, in which case use 1 teaspoon of the dried leaves for every cup of water. Once the decoction has cooled slightly, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool completely before using it as an eye wash or compress. To use as an eye wash, gently pour the cooled decoction over your closed eyes, and let it sit for 2-3 minutes before rinsing with clean water. For a compress, soak a clean cloth in the cooled decoction, wring it out, and place it over your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes. Repeat as needed, but always wash your hands before handling the plant or applying the decoction to your eyes.

intro_desc: Itchy eyes can be a real nuisance, causing discomfort and distracting you from daily activities. If left untreated, it can lead to eye infections, vision problems, or even long-term damage. One effective way to address itchy eyes is through herbal decoctions. Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, is particularly useful in soothing and calming irritated eyes. Its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and redness, while its antibacterial properties combat underlying infections.  Calendula officinalis, or marigold, is another beneficial herb in treating itchy eyes. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties work together to soothe the eyes and prevent further irritation. Calendula's ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation can also help to repair any damage to the eye tissues. By addressing the root cause of itchy eyes, herbal decoctions can provide relief and prevent further complications.  Fixing itchy eyes is essential to maintaining eye health and preventing long-term damage. When your eyes are constantly irritated, it can affect your ability to focus and concentrate, impacting daily activities and productivity. Itchy eyes can also lead to stress and anxiety, further exacerbating the issue. By addressing itchy eyes with herbal decoctions, you can improve your overall well-being and quality of life. Regular use of herbal decoctions can help to prevent future episodes of itchy eyes, giving you peace of mind and freedom to engage in daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_best: To remedy itchy eyes, the best combination of herbal decoctions is made from Euphrasia officinalis and Chamomilla recutita. Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, is specifically used to treat eye problems, including itchy eyes. It contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the eyes.  Chamomilla recutita, or German chamomile, is another herb that is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It contains apigenin and luteolin, which help to calm the eyes and reduce redness. When combined, these two herbs create a powerful decoction that targets the root cause of itchy eyes, providing relief and comfort to the eyes.  Using other herbs from the list may not be as effective because they do not specifically target eye problems. For example, Calendula officinalis is used to treat skin problems and Aloe vera is used to soothe burns, but they are not specifically designed to treat itchy eyes. Echinacea purpurea is used to boost the immune system and Symphytum officinale is used to treat bone and joint problems, but they are not directly related to eye health.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy eyes, it's best to avoid herbal decoctions that can irritate or exacerbate the condition. One such herb is Eyebright's cousin, 'Borago officinalis' (Borage), also known as 'Borago', which can cause eye irritation and discomfort in some individuals.  Another herb to avoid is 'Ruta graveolens' (Rue), which can cause photophobia, eye pain, and even vision problems in high doses. Its active compounds can also cause irritation and redness in the eyes.  Additionally, 'Cinchona officinalis' (Cinchona) and 'Hydrastis canadensis' (Goldenseal) should be used with caution due to their potential to cause eye irritation and allergic reactions in some people.  While 'Artemisia absinthium' (Wormwood) is generally used to treat digestive issues, its essential oils can cause eye irritation, so it's best to avoid it in decoctions meant for eye relief.  It's also worth noting that 'Zanthoxylum bungeanum' (Sichuan Pepper) and 'Zanthoxylum clavatum' (Sichuan Pepper) contain compounds that can cause eye irritation and should be used with caution in eye treatments.  Lastly, 'Rheum officinale' (Rhubarb) and 'Cassia auriculata' (Tulsi) can cause eye irritation in some individuals and should be avoided in eye treatments.
supplementary_other_remedies: Itchy eyes can be a real nuisance, and while herbal decoctions can be helpful, there are other natural remedies that can provide relief. One such remedy is using a warm compress. Soaking a cloth in warm water, wringing it out, and applying it to the affected eye can help loosen and clear out any irritants that may be causing the itchiness. This is because the warmth helps to increase blood flow to the area, which in turn helps to reduce swelling and ease any discomfort.  Another remedy is to try using a saltwater rinse. Dissolving a small amount of salt in warm water creates a solution that can be used to gently flush out any debris or irritants that may be causing the itchiness. The salt helps to reduce inflammation and kill any bacteria that may be present, which can help to soothe and calm the eyes.  Aloe vera gel can also be used to help soothe itchy eyes. The gel has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease any discomfort. Applying a small amount of aloe vera gel to the affected eye can help to provide quick relief from itchiness.  Additionally, some people find that using a humidifier can help to relieve itchy eyes. Dry air can exacerbate itchiness, so using a humidifier can help to add moisture back into the air, which can help to soothe and calm the eyes.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have itchy eyes are often plagued by other eye problems and allergic reactions. They may experience redness, swelling, and watery discharge, making it hard to see and feel comfortable. One common issue is dry eyes, caused by a lack of moisture on the surface of the eye. This can be treated with medicinal herbs like aloe vera, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize the eyes. Another problem is conjunctivitis, an infection that causes pink eye, which can be treated with herbs like calendula, known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Some people may also experience itchiness on the skin around their eyes, which could be a sign of eczema or dermatitis, conditions that can be managed with herbs like chamomile, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
